title: Navigation History, Immutable Snapshots, Back, and Bounded Undo · Type-System Trace: Pokedex Ts History Model Tests
summary: History stores mutable references, pushes attempts before success, back triggers new hidden network work, unlimited pages accumulate, and undo can resurrect a stale pending request. Predict the user decision, runtime value, strict type claim, promise state, API boundary, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex ts history model tests increment in bounded immutable navigation timeline. Learners progress through prediction,…
objectives: Generate command sequences and verify cursor, identity, ordering, bounds, stale-result exclusion, and state after every transition.
Bound history by entries or bytes and define eviction, collection interaction, and persistence behavior visibly.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Pokedex Ts History Model Tests
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript team handles case 9815 during a shared classroom network exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to verify immutable successful-view history, offline back, stale exclusion, and capacity bounds; no user-visible meaning may rely on color, cursor motion, or pointer input; then inject one redirect, prototype-like key, terminal control, path, file, or signal failure and reject it safely. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
